code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test19-JAN-2024Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), all supporting possible renal dysfunction, likely of tubular origin. No evidence of urinary tract infection is present. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for underlying causes such as diabetic or hypertensive nephropathy.

coding.display: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
